seo.title: MRI Prostate at Cadabams Diagnostics | Advanced, Safe Scan
seo.description: MRI Prostate: The gold-standard scan for detecting prostate cancer, BPH, and related issues. Painless and radiation-free. Book at Cadabams today.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Prostate Overview

### What is MRI Prostate?
\*\*MRI Prostate\*\* is a specialised magnetic-resonance scan that creates detailed 3-D pictures of the prostate gland and surrounding tissue. Unlike basic ultrasound, it uses powerful magnets and radio waves—\*\*no radiation\*\*—to reveal problems as small as a few millimetres.

## Risks and Limitations of the MRI of Prostate Scan

- \*\*Extremely safe\*\*: No radiation, no known tissue damage
- \*\*Contrast allergy\*\*: Rare reaction to gadolinium dye—tell the technician about kidney issues or past allergies
- \*\*Claustrophobia\*\*: Open-bore MRI machines at Cadabams Diagnostics reduce anxiety
- \*\*Limitation\*\*: Very early, microscopic cancer cells may still need a biopsy for confirmation

## Parameters in a Multiparametric MRI Prostate Scan

| Parameter | What It Shows |
|-----------|---------------|
| \*\*T1 & T2-weighted sequences\*\* | Gland anatomy, presence of bleeding or calcification |
| \*\*Diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI)\*\* | How tightly packed cells are—cancer often restricts water movement |
| \*\*Dynamic contrast-enhanced (DCE) imaging\*\* | Blood-flow patterns; tumours usually enhance faster than normal tissue |

## Reasons for choosing the MRI Prostate Scan

- \*\*Detecting prostate cancer\*\* – Locate suspicious lesions and decide if biopsy is needed
- \*\*Evaluating benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)\*\* – Measure prostate volume before surgery
- \*\*Monitoring post-treatment changes\*\* – Check for cancer recurrence or shrinking after radiation or surgery

## Benefits of an MRI Prostate Scan

- \*\*High-resolution soft-tissue contrast\*\* – Differentiates tumour from normal tissue better than any other scan
- \*\*No radiation exposure\*\* – Safe for repeat imaging
- \*\*Guides targeted biopsies\*\* – Helps the doctor take fewer, more accurate samples

## Conditions Diagnosed with a Prostate MRI Scan

| Condition | How MRI Helps |
|-----------|---------------|
| \*\*Prostate cancer\*\* | Pinpoints location, size, spread beyond capsule |
| \*\*Prostatitis\*\* | Highlights diffuse or focal inflammation |
| \*\*Prostatic abscess\*\* | Shows fluid-filled cavity needing drainage |

## How to Prepare for a Prostate MRI Scan

### Pre-requisites
- \*\*Remove metal\*\* – Watches, rings, belts and credit cards
- \*\*Empty bladder\*\* 30 minutes before the scan for clearer images
- \*\*Inform staff\*\* about pacemakers, stents or previous contrast reactions
### Best Time to Take the MRI Prostate
Any day is suitable. If you are on antibiotics for infection, complete the course first to avoid false inflammation signals.
### Eligibility
- \*\*Age\*\*: 18 years and above
- \*\*Weight limit\*\*: Up to 150 kg on our open-bore scanner
- \*\*Contra-indications\*\*: MRI-incompatible pacemakers, cochlear implants or certain aneurysm clips
### Procedure for Taking an MRI Prostate
1. Change into cotton gown
2. Lie on the table feet-first; a small coil rests over the pelvis
3. \*\*Contrast injection\*\* (if mpMRI) via IV line midway through the scan
4. Stay still; each sequence lasts 3–5 minutes; total time ≈ 25–35 minutes
5. Technician speaks to you via intercom throughout
### Caution Before Taking the Test
- \*\*Sedation\*\*: Mild oral relaxant available on request—arrange someone to drive you home
- \*\*Diabetes\*\*: Hold Metformin 48 hours if contrast is used
- \*\*Kidney function\*\*: Recent creatinine blood test required for contrast studies

seo.description: Get a high-resolution MRI scan of tongue at Cadabams Diagnostics. No radiation, same-day reports, and expert radiologists. Book your slot now!

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Scan of Tongue Overview

### What the test checks
- Maps tumour size, depth and spread
- Shows lymph-node involvement
- Differentiates cancer from benign lumps, cysts or infection
### Why choose Cadabams Diagnostics
- 3T MRI + specialised head-neck coil
- Sub-24-hour report turnaround
- Online booking, digital reports, and transparent pricing

## Risks & Limitations of the MRI Scan of Tongue

### Contraindications
- Pacemaker / defibrillator (most models)
- Cochlear implants, neuro-stimulators
- Certain aneurysm clips
### Claustrophobia considerations
Machine bore is 60–70 cm wide; exam lasts 20–40 min. Open MRI or mild sedation can be arranged on request.

## List of Parameters Considered During the MRI Scan of Tongue

- Tongue thickness and symmetry
- Depth of invasion (DOI) – key cancer-staging metric
- Signal pattern: solid vs. necrotic
- Lymph-node size, number and extracapsular spread
- Involvement of floor of mouth, mandible, neuro-vascular bundles

## Reasons for Taking an MRI Scan of Tongue

- Staging malignant lesions (TNM classification)
- Pre-surgical mapping – save critical nerves and vessels
- Post-treatment follow-up – separate scar from recurrence
- Evaluation of tongue-tie or implant planning (selected cases)

## Benefits of an MRI of Tongue Scan

- Zero radiation – ideal for children, young adults and serial scans
- Multi-planar views – true 3-D understanding
- High soft-tissue contrast – detect 2 mm lesions
- Same-day digital report – faster therapy decisions

## Illnesses Diagnosed with MRI Scan of Tongue

- Tongue squamous cell carcinoma (90% of tongue cancers)
- Benign tumours – granular cell tumour, schwannoma, lipoma
- Vascular malformations & cysts
- Abscess, cellulitis, non-infectious inflammation (e.g., sarcoidosis)

## Preparing for MRI Scan of Tongue

- Fasting 4 hours if contrast is planned; water is allowed.
- Remove all jewellery, hairpins, hearing aids.
- Inform staff about pregnancy, allergies, prior contrast reaction.
- Bring previous scans/CDs for comparison.
## Pre-requisites
- Written prescription from ENT / oncologist / physician
- Recent kidney function test (Creatinine/eGFR) within 4 weeks if gadolinium is expected
- Diabetic patients on metformin may need to withhold drug on the day of scan.
## Best Time to Take the MRI Scan of Tongue
- Early-morning slots minimise waiting and claustrophobia.
- Avoid acute throat infections – motion artefacts from swallowing.
- Women in first trimester may defer unless urgent.
## Eligibility
- Adults of any age
- Children ≥5 years (younger kids need sedation)
- Pregnant patients – non-contrast MRI only when benefits outweigh risks
## Procedure for Taking an MRI Scan of Tongue
1. Change into metal-free gown.
2. Lie supine; head-neck coil positioned like a helmet.
3. Foam pads minimise movement.
4. Contrast injection (if ordered) via IV line.
5. Scanner table moves in; you hear knocking sounds – ear-plugs provided.
6. Stay still; occasional breath-hold commands.
7. Total table time: 20–40 minutes.
8. IV line removed; dressing applied.
9. You may leave immediately unless sedated.
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- Remove credit cards, phones – magnet can corrupt data.
- Notify technologist about tattoos, permanent eyeliner (some contain iron).
- Renal impairment? Contrast dose is adjusted or withheld.

## MRI Scrotum Overview

- \*\*Quick definition:\*\* A non-invasive scan that uses magnetic fields and radio waves to create detailed 3D images of the scrotum, testicles, and surrounding structures.
- \*\*Purpose:\*\* Detect tumours, inflammation, torsion, or causes of male infertility.
- \*\*Why Cadabams?\*\* 3T MRI technology, experienced radiologists, and zero-radiation scans—all under one roof.

## Risks and Limitations of the MRI Scan of the Scrotum

- \*\*No radiation risk\*\*—ideal for younger patients.
- \*\*Claustrophobia\*\*: Inform us; open-bore or mild sedation options available.
- \*\*Contra-indications\*\*: Pacemakers, cochlear implants, or certain metallic implants.
- \*\*Cost\*\* slightly higher than ultrasound, but justified by superior diagnostic accuracy.

## Imaging Parameters in a Scrotum MRI Scan

- \*\*Testicular volume & signal intensity\*\*
- \*\*Epididymal morphology\*\*
- \*\*Vascular flow\*\* (on mpMRI)
- \*\*Presence of masses or fluid collections\*\*

## Reasons for Taking an MRI Scrotum

- Clarify \*\*inconclusive ultrasound results\*\*
- \*\*Stage testicular tumours\*\*
- Assess \*\*undescended testis\*\* in adults
- Evaluate \*\*chronic scrotal pain\*\*

## Benefits of a Scrotum MRI Radiology Scan

- \*\*High-resolution 3D images\*\*
- \*\*Soft-tissue contrast\*\* superior to ultrasound
- Guides \*\*surgery or biopsy decisions\*\*
- \*\*Same-day digital reports\*\* at Cadabams

## Conditions Diagnosed with an MRI of the Scrotum Scan

| Condition | How MRI Helps |
|----------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Testicular cancer | Accurate staging & tumour characterisation |
| Epididymo-orchitis | Detects inflammation and abscesses |
| Varicocele | Maps dilated veins |
| Testicular torsion | Identifies sequelae after torsion |
| Hydrocele & spermatocele | Shows fluid collections precisely |

## How to Prepare for a Scrotum MRI Scan

- \*\*Fasting\*\*: 4–6 hours if contrast is planned.
- \*\*Metal screening\*\*: Fill a short questionnaire.
- \*\*Clothing\*\*: Wear metal-free, comfortable attire.
---
## Pre-requisites
- Doctor’s prescription
- Recent \*\*creatinine test\*\* if contrast is used
- \*\*Insurance pre-authorisation\*\* (optional)
---
## Best Time to Take the MRI Scrotum
- \*\*Morning slots\*\* reduce waiting time.
- Book within \*\*7 days of doctor’s referral\*\* for faster turnaround and priority reporting.
---
## Eligibility
- \*\*Males 12 years and above\*\*
- \*\*Not suitable for pregnant women\*\* (rare indication)
---
## Procedure for Taking an MRI Scrotum
1. \*\*30–45 min scan time\*\*.
2. You lie on the scanner table; a cushioned coil is placed over the scrotum.
3. \*\*Contrast injection\*\* (if mpMRI) through a small IV line.
4. \*\*Breath-hold instructions\*\* for motion-free images.
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- Remove \*\*all metallic objects\*\* (watches, belts, jewellery).
- Inform the technologist about \*\*tattoos\*\*—some inks contain metallic pigments.
- \*\*Sedation options\*\* available for claustrophobia.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Sternoclavicular Joint Overview

### What is an MRI Sternoclavicular Joint scan?
A painless, radiation-free magnetic resonance imaging study focused on the \*\*SC joint anatomy\*\*—cartilage, ligaments, bone marrow, and nearby soft tissues.
### Key differences from CT & X-ray of the sternum
- \*\*X-ray:\*\* Shows bone fractures or dislocations but misses early marrow changes and soft-tissue injury.
- \*\*CT:\*\* Excellent for complex fractures, yet involves ionising radiation and poor soft-tissue contrast.
- \*\*MRI:\*\* Superior for \*\*soft-tissue inflammation\*\*, \*\*ligament integrity\*\*, marrow oedema, and early infection—without radiation.

## Risks and Limitations of the Sterno-Clavicular Joints MRI Scan

### MRI safety & contraindications
- \*\*Absolute:\*\* Cardiac pacemaker, certain aneurysm clips, cochlear implants.
- \*\*Relative:\*\* Recent surgery clips, claustrophobia (we offer wide-bore scanners and mild sedation).
### Limitations in certain metal implants or claustrophobia
Metal near the joint (e.g., sternal wires from open-heart surgery) can distort images. Our technologists review every implant with you beforehand.

## List of Parameters Considered During the Sterno-Clavicular Joints MRI Scan

- Soft-tissue inflammation
- Ligament integrity (anterior, posterior, costoclavicular)
- Bone marrow edema (early fracture or infection)
- Sternum alignment and symmetry
- Joint space effusion or synovial thickening

## Reasons for Taking a Sterno-Clavicular Joints MRI Scan

- \*\*Trauma & dislocation:\*\* High-impact sports, seat-belt injuries
- \*\*Infection (septic arthritis):\*\* Rapid swelling, fever
- \*\*Inflammatory arthritis:\*\* Rheumatoid, psoriatic, or ankylosing spondylitis
- \*\*Tumours & cysts:\*\* Osteochondromas, metastases, ganglion cysts

## Benefits of a Sterno-Clavicular Joints MRI Scan

- \*\*High-resolution 3T imaging\*\*—double the clarity of 1.5T machines
- \*\*Fast scan protocols\*\*—complete study in 20-30 min
- \*\*Expert radiology reporting\*\*—sub-specialist musculoskeletal radiologists
- \*\*Flexible slots\*\*—morning, evening, weekend bookings
- \*\*Same-day report\*\* option for urgent cases

## Illnesses Diagnosed with Sterno-Clavicular Joints MRI Scan

- Sternoclavicular joint pathology (sprain, subluxation)
- Sternal fractures & osteomyelitis
- Rheumatoid & seronegative arthritis
- Synovitis, capsulitis, and rare PVNS (pigmented villonodular synovitis)

## Preparing for Sterno-Clavicular Joints MRI Scan

- \*\*Metal screening:\*\* Remove jewellery, hearing aids, hairpins, and tell us about any implants.
- \*\*Fasting:\*\* Not required; eat and drink normally.
- \*\*Best time to scan:\*\* Any time; no menstrual or cycle dependency.
- \*\*Eligibility:\*\* Adults and children >5 years (paediatric sedation available).
---
## Procedure for MRI Sternoclavicular Joint
1. \*\*Arrival & registration\*\* – 10 min
2. \*\*Safety questionnaire & changing\*\* – 5 min
3. \*\*Positioning\*\* – You lie on your back with arms at your sides; a small coil rests over your chest.
4. \*\*Scanning\*\* – 20-30 min; you’ll hear loud knocking sounds (earplugs provided).
5. \*\*Post-scan instructions\*\* – Resume normal activities immediately; no recovery time.
---
## Cautions Before the Test
- \*\*Pacemaker & implant checks:\*\* Bring device cards or discharge summaries.
- \*\*Contrast allergy screening:\*\* Gadolinium is rarely needed, but inform us of prior reactions or kidney issues.
- \*\*Pregnancy:\*\* First-trimester scans deferred unless critical.

faqs.question: Are there any side effects from the MRI scan?
faqs.answer:
MRI is considered very safe with no known long-term side effects. The most common issues are related to claustrophobia or, very rarely, a mild allergic reaction to the contrast agent. Our team at Cadabam's Diagnostics is fully prepared to manage any such concerns.

If you have more questions about your upcoming scan, please don't hesitate to reach out to our team. For a comprehensive overview of our imaging services.

seo.title: MRI Temporal Bones at Cadabams Diagnostics | Safe & Precise
seo.description: Learn everything about MRI Temporal Bones—why it’s done, how it works, benefits, risks, and how to prepare. Book your scan at Cadabams Diagnostics today.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Temporal Bones Overview

### Definition & Purpose
- \*\*MRI Temporal Bones\*\* is a high-resolution magnetic resonance study focused on the temporal bone, inner ear, and surrounding nerves.
- It shows nerves, blood vessels, and soft tissue far better than traditional imaging.
### How It Differs from CT & X-ray
| Imaging Type | Radiation | Best For |
|--------------|-----------|----------|
| X-ray | Yes | Quick fracture check |
| CT | Yes | Bone detail |
| MRI Temporal Bones | Zero | Nerves, tumors, infection |

## Risks and Limitations of the MRI Temporal Bone Radiology Scan

| Risk | How We Handle It |
|------|------------------|
| Pacemaker / Implant | Step-by-step metal screening before entry |
| Claustrophobia | Open-bore MRI & optional mild sedation |
| Missed bony detail | If bone erosion is suspected, we may recommend a follow-up CT |

## Imaging Parameters in a Temporal Bones MRI Scan

- \*\*Cochlear nerve\*\* integrity and diameter
- \*\*Vestibular apparatus\*\* and semicircular canals
- \*\*Facial nerve canal\*\*, labyrinthine segment & tympanic segment
- \*\*Middle ear cavity\*\*, ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes)
- \*\*Mastoid air cells\*\* for fluid or inflammation

## Reasons for Taking an MRI Temporal Bones

- \*\*Unexplained sensorineural hearing loss\*\*
- \*\*Recurrent vertigo\*\* or imbalance unresponsive to medication
- \*\*Suspected acoustic neuroma\*\* or schwannoma pressing on nerves

## Benefits of a Temporal Bone MRI Test

- \*\*Zero radiation\*\* – safe for children and repeated follow-ups
- \*\*Superior soft-tissue contrast\*\* – finds 1–2 mm lesions earlier
- \*\*Early detection\*\* leads to better outcomes and hearing preservation

## Conditions Diagnosed with an MRI of Temporal Bone Radiology Scan

| Condition | What MRI Shows |
|-----------|----------------|
| Vestibular schwannoma | Enhancing mass in internal auditory canal |
| Cholesteatoma | Soft-tissue sac eroding bone |
| Otosclerosis | Abnormal spongy bone around cochlea |
| Congenital anomalies | Cochlear malformations, enlarged vestibular aqueduct |

## How to Prepare for an MRI of Temporal Bone Scan

- \*\*Fasting\*\*: Not required for non-contrast; 4-hour fast if contrast is planned.
- \*\*Medications\*\*: Take all routine drugs with small sips of water.
- \*\*Metal screening\*\*: Remove jewellery, hearing aids, hairpins; inform staff about pacemakers, cochlear implants, or tattoos.
- \*\*Clothing\*\*: Wear metal-free cotton garments or change into our provided gown.
## Pre-requisites
- Doctor’s prescription or referral note
- Previous imaging reports (CT, prior MRI) for comparison
- Recent \*\*renal function test\*\* (creatinine) if gadolinium contrast is needed
## Best Time to Take the Scan
- \*\*Early morning slots\*\* reduce wait times and help claustrophobic patients feel calmer.
- \*\*Avoid acute ear infections\*\*; reschedule if you have active pain or discharge.
## Eligibility
- \*\*Adults & children ≥5 years\*\*
- \*\*Pregnancy\*\*: 2nd or 3rd trimester only if essential; no contrast given
## Step-by-Step Procedure
1. \*\*Registration & safety screening\*\* (~10 min)
2. \*\*Positioning\*\*: Lie on your back; head secured in a soft coil (~5 min)
3. \*\*Scan\*\*: 25–35 min of intermittent loud knocking; earplugs provided
4. \*\*Contrast injection\*\* (if ordered) via IV line during the scan
5. \*\*Post-scan observation\*\* (~15 min) for any delayed reaction
## Caution Before the Test
- List \*\*all implants\*\*: joint replacements, stents, IUDs
- Report \*\*tattoos\*\* with metallic ink; may warm up
- Ask about \*\*sedation\*\* if you have severe claustrophobia

seo.title: MRI Temporomandibular Joints (TMJ): Accurate Scan
seo.description: Book an MRI TMJ scan at Cadabams Diagnostics for detailed 3D jaw-joint imaging, no radiation, and same-day digital reports for accurate diagnosis.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI – Temporomandibular Joints (TMJ) Overview

### What is an MRI Temporomandibular Joints scan?
An MRI Temporomandibular Joints study is a non-invasive test that creates ultra-clear 3D pictures of the jaw joint (TMJ), surrounding soft-tissues, disc, and bone. It shows how the disc moves when you open and close your mouth—helping doctors detect early arthritis, disc slippage, or inflammation.
### Why choose Cadabams Diagnostics for TMJ MRI?
- 3-Tesla magnet for sub-millimetre resolution
- Quiet, wide-bore scanner lowers claustrophobia
- Dynamic sequences that image jaw motion
- Reports within 2 hours; urgent scans on request
- Direct online booking and dedicated TMJ protocols designed by senior radiologists

## Risks & Limitations of MRI Temporomandibular Joint

- \*\*No radiation risk\*\*—safe for children and repeat follow-ups
- \*\*Claustrophobia\*\*: our wide-bore scanner and mirror goggles reduce anxiety; mild sedation available
- \*\*Metal implants\*\*: pacemakers, certain aneurysm clips, cochlear implants may be contraindicated—notify staff during booking

## Parameters Considered during MRI Temporomandibular Joint Scan

- Disc position (normal, anterior, posterior)
- Disc morphology (biconcave, deformed, perforated)
- Condylar head integrity (erosion, osteophytes)
- Joint effusion & synovial inflammation
- Surrounding muscles (masseter, temporalis) for fatty atrophy

## Reasons for the MRI Temporomandibular Joint Test

- Confirm \*\*internal derangement\*\* with or without reduction
- Detect early \*\*TMJ osteoarthritis\*\* before bone loss is visible on X-ray
- Identify inflammatory arthritides (rheumatoid, gout, psoriasis)
- Plan orthodontic surgery, arthrocentesis, or prosthetic joint placement

## Benefits of the MRI Temporomandibular Joint Scan

- \*\*High-resolution soft-tissue detail\*\*—see discs, ligaments, cartilage
- \*\*Early detection\*\* prevents irreversible joint damage
- \*\*Objective images\*\* guide targeted therapy: splints, physiotherapy, injections
- \*\*No radiation\*\* allows follow-ups in growing children

## Conditions Diagnosed during MRI Temporomandibular Joint Test

1. Disc displacement with/without reduction
2. TMJ osteoarthritis & subchondral cysts
3. Rheumatoid & psoriatic arthritis
4. Synovial chondromatosis
5. Avascular necrosis of condyle
6. Tumours (synovial sarcoma, chondroblastoma)—rare

## Preparation for MRI Temporomandibular Joint

- Remove jewellery, hearing aids, hairpins
- Wear cotton gown; no special fasting needed
- Inform staff about tattoos, permanent eyeliner, copper IUDs
- Bring implant card for clips, plates, or prosthetics
---
## Pre-requisites
- Written referral from ENT, dentist, orthodontist, or oral surgeon
- Earlier X-ray, CT, or MRI films (CD or cloud link) for comparison
---
## Best Time to Take the MRI Temporomandibular Joints
- \*\*Morning slots\*\* help claustrophobic patients feel fresh
- \*\*Same-day urgent scans\*\* available for trauma or medico-legal cases
- Menstruation does not affect imaging; pregnancy—consult radiologist first
---
## Eligibility
- Adults and cooperative children ≥ 5 years (younger kids may need sedation)
- Pregnant patients: MRI is safe, yet first-trimester scans require risk-benefit review
---
## Procedure for Taking an MRI Temporomandibular Joints
1. \*\*Check-in & safety screening\*\* (5 min)
2. Change into gown, store valuables in secure locker
3. Lie supine; technologist places TMJ coil over both sides
4. \*\*20–30 min scan\*\*: you’ll hear knocking sounds; keep still
5. Immediate preliminary report; final signed report within 2 hours
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- Notify staff about coloured tattoos that may heat up
- Inform if you wear insulin pump or neuro-stimulator
- \*\*Sedation options\*\*: oral benzodiazepine or IV short-acting anaesthetic; arrange an escort home

interpretations.title: Results and Interpretations
rows: An area of bright signal within the bone marrow on fluid-sensitive sequences.
This is a non-specific sign of stress. It can indicate a hidden fracture, bone bruise, inflammation from arthritis, or infection.
Brightness of the joint lining (synovium) after the injection of contrast dye.
Suggests active inflammation (synovitis), a hallmark of conditions like rheumatoid arthritis or an infection.
A well-defined, fluid-filled sac seen adjacent to a joint or tendon sheath.
Most commonly represents a ganglion cyst. Other masses would be described by their size, location, and characteristics to determine if they are benign or require further evaluation.

seo.title: MRI Thumb at Cadabams Diagnostics | Book 3T Scan Today
seo.description: Get a high-resolution MRI Thumb scan in 20–30 min at Cadabams Diagnostics. 3T magnet, same-day report, transparent pricing. Book now.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI of the Thumb an Overview

\*\*MRI thumb\*\* is a specialized \*\*Magnetic Resonance Imaging\*\* exam that zeroes in on:
- Thumb bones (distal phalanx, proximal phalanx, first metacarpal)
- Ligaments—especially the \*\*ulnar collateral ligament (UCL)\*\*
- Tendons such as \*\*EPL\*\* and \*\*FPL\*\*
- Surrounding soft tissues and joint capsule
### How It Works
A strong magnetic field and radio waves create \*\*high-contrast cross-sectional images\*\*. The result? Your doctor sees injuries that \*\*X-rays often miss\*\*, guiding the best treatment path.

## Risks and Limitations of MRI Thumb Scan

### Safety
- \*\*No radiation\*\*—safe for repeated imaging
- Contrast dye (gadolinium) is \*\*low-risk\*\* and used only when needed
### Contraindications
- Pacemaker or non-MRI-safe implants
- Severe claustrophobia (we offer open-bore 3T and sedation)
### Limitations
- May miss \*\*tiny cortical fractures\*\*—a follow-up \*\*CT scan\*\* can complement the findings.

## MRI Thumb Parameters Considered

- \*\*FOV:\*\* 140 × 70 mm SFOV optimized for thumb anatomy
- \*\*Slice thickness:\*\* 2 mm axial, coronal, sagittal
- \*\*Sequences:\*\* T1 TSE, T2 STIR, PD fat-sat
- \*\*Matrix:\*\* 256 × 192 for crisp detail
- \*\*TR/TE:\*\* Tailored to our 3T magnet specs for \*\*maximum clarity\*\*

## Reasons for an MRI of the Thumb

- Detect \*\*ligament tears\*\* (UCL, RCL)
- Evaluate \*\*tendon injuries\*\* (EPL, FPL)
- Spot \*\*occult fractures or bone bruises\*\*
- Assess \*\*early arthritis or synovitis\*\*

## MRI of Thumb Benefits:

- \*\*Non-invasive & radiation-free\*\*
- \*\*Superior soft-tissue contrast\*\*—see what X-rays can’t
- Directly \*\*guides surgical vs. conservative treatment\*\*
- \*\*Digital report with annotated images\*\* delivered same day

## Conditions Diagnosed with MRI for Thumb

- \*\*Gamekeeper’s / skier’s thumb\*\* (UCL tear)
- \*\*De Quervain’s tenosynovitis\*\*
- \*\*Scaphoid fracture\*\* missed on X-ray
- \*\*Basal thumb arthritis\*\*
- \*\*Flexor/extensor tendon ruptures\*\*

## Preparation for MRI Thumb Scan

- \*\*Remove all metallic objects\*\*—jewelry, watches, belts
- Wear \*\*metal-free clothing\*\* or change into our gown
- \*\*Inform staff\*\* about implants, pregnancy, or claustrophobia
- \*\*Fasting not required\*\*—eat normally unless contrast is planned
---
## Pre-requisites
- Referral note from \*\*orthopedician or GP\*\*
- Bring \*\*previous imaging reports\*\* for comparison
---
## Best Time to Take the MRI Thumb
- \*\*Within 1–2 weeks of acute injury\*\* for optimal soft-tissue detail
- \*\*Morning slots\*\* reduce waiting time and fit busy schedules
---
## Eligibility
- \*\*Adults and children >5 years\*\* (cooperation permitting)
- \*\*Pregnancy:\*\* Second or third trimester only if clinically essential
---
## Step-by-Step Procedure at Cadabams Diagnostics
1. \*\*Check-in & Screening\*\* (5 min)
2. \*\*Positioning:\*\* Hand palm-up in dedicated thumb coil
3. \*\*Scanning:\*\* 20–30 min with intermittent instructions
4. \*\*Radiologist review\*\* before you leave
---
## Caution Before the Test
- Disclose \*\*tattoos with metallic ink\*\*—they may heat up
- \*\*Sedation available\*\* for severe claustrophobia (request in advance)

seo.title: MRI Whole Body at Cadabams Diagnostics | Book Today
seo.description: Book a no-radiation MRI Whole Body scan at Cadabams Diagnostics for early detection of cancers, aneurysms, and more with quick digital reports.

relatedTests.markdown:
## Whole Body MRI – An Overview

### What the whole body MRI package covers
- High-resolution images of 6 major body regions
- 1.5 Tesla Siemens scanner for crisp detail
- Complimentary radiologist consult & 24-hour digital report
### Why Cadabams Diagnostics is preferred
- 35+ years of diagnostics legacy in South India
- Same-day appointment flexibility
- Transparent, all-inclusive pricing—no hidden contrast charges
## What Is MRI Whole Body?
### Definition and technology behind whole body MRI
Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) uses powerful magnets and radio waves to create cross-sectional images. In a \*\*MRI Whole Body\*\*, the table moves continuously while multiple coils capture data, stitching together a full anatomical map.
### Difference from targeted or contrast MRI
- No dye injection in the standard protocol—ideal for screening
- Covers entire body rather than one organ
- Zero ionising radiation compared with CT whole body scans

## Risks & Limitations of MRI Whole Body Scan

### Low-risk procedure with no radiation exposure
- MRI is regarded as one of the safest imaging modalities
- No known biological hazards at 1.5 T field strength
### Limitations: small-lesion detection, claustrophobia, metal implants
- Lesions <5 mm may need targeted follow-up
- Tight scanner bore can trigger anxiety (open-bore option available)
- Pacemakers, cochlear implants, certain aneurysm clips are contraindicated

## MRI Whole Body Parameters Considered

### Brain, spine, and musculoskeletal review
- White-matter changes, disc herniations, marrow infiltration
### Chest & abdominal organs, pelvis, soft-tissue masses
- Liver, pancreas, kidneys, prostate/uterus, lymph-node stations

## Reasons for Whole Body MRI Scan

### Early detection of tumours, aneurysms, inflammation
- Spot solid-organ cancers at Stage 1
- Identify aortic aneurysms before rupture
### Baseline health mapping before lifestyle changes
- Document pre-diet visceral fat levels
- Compare annual progress objectively

## Benefits of Whole Body MRI

- Single-session full-body scan—no need for multiple appointments
- No radiation, high-resolution imaging
- Digital report within 24 hours—share instantly with any specialist

## Conditions Diagnosed with Whole Body MRI

- Solid-organ cancers: liver, kidney, pancreas, prostate, breast
- Vascular anomalies: aneurysms, dissections, AVMs
- Degenerative spine disease: canal stenosis, disc prolapse

## Preparation for Whole Body MRI Scan

### Pre-requisites: fasting 4 hrs, remove metal objects
- Light breakfast allowed if booked after 12 pm
- Leave jewellery, belts and watches at home
### Best time: morning slots reduce waiting
- Centre opens 7 am; finish scan before office hours
### Eligibility: 18+ yrs, BMI ≤35, no pacemaker
- Weight limit 120 kg for table safety
## Procedure for Taking a MRI Whole Body
1. Change into cotton gown; locker for valuables
2. Lie supine on MRI table; cushions stabilise head & knees
3. Coil positioning: head, neck, body phased-array
4. Table moves in 8 stations; machine makes knocking sounds (ear plugs provided)
5. Total table time: 45-60 minutes
6. Radiologist reviews images; you leave with a provisional summary
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- Inform staff about implants, pregnancy, claustrophobia
- Open-bore or 3 T wide-bore alternatives available on request

rows: Fraying and partial-thickness contrast imbibition on the articular surface of the distal supraspinatus tendon.

Early chondromalacia or degenerative change – may correlate with chronic joint pain or wear.
The long head of the biceps tendon origin and its labral anchor appear intact. No evidence of tear or subluxation.
Normal finding – stable biceps-labral complex, no signs of injury or detachment.

MRI-related risks are primarily for individuals with incompatible metallic implants or severe claustrophobia. Our team at Cadabams Diagnostics takes every precaution to ensure your safety.

seo.title: MRI Arthrogram at Cadabams Diagnostics | Accurate & Same-Day
seo.description: MRI Arthrogram at Cadabams: Learn why it's ordered, patient preparation, and how it delivers high-resolution joint imaging with fast, same-day reports.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Arthrogram Scan Overview

### MRI Arthrogram vs Standard MRI
• \*\*Standard MRI\*\* uses magnetic fields and radio waves to create images.
• \*\*MRI Arthrogram\*\* adds a safe contrast dye injected directly into the joint, outlining soft tissues like cartilage, ligaments, and labrum that a regular MRI can miss.

## When and Who Needs to Take an MRI Arthrogram Scan

• Persistent joint pain not explained by x-ray or ultrasound
• Athletes with suspected labral or rotator-cuff tears
• Patients facing arthroscopic surgery who need precise mapping

## Risks & Limitations of the MRI Arthrogram Scan

• Rare allergic reaction to contrast (gadolinium)
• Slight infection risk at injection site
• Not suitable for pregnant women or patients with severe kidney disease

## List of Parameters Considered During the MRI Arthrogram Scan

• Capsule thickness
• Labral shape and signal
• Cartilage thickness and integrity
• Ligament continuity
• Presence of fluid leaks or tears

## Reasons for Taking an MRI Arthrogram Scan

\*\*Shoulder pain & rotator cuff injuries\*\* – pinpoints partial or full-thickness tears
\*\*Hip labral tears\*\* – reveals fraying or detachment causing groin pain
\*\*Knee ligament or cartilage damage\*\* – measures meniscus, ACL, PCL status
\*\*Wrist and ankle instability\*\* – clarifies ligament sprains or cartilage defects

## Benefits of Taking the Test

\*\*High-resolution joint detail\*\* – lesions as small as 1–2 mm visible
\*\*Accurate surgical planning\*\* – surgeons use the images as a “road map”
\*\*Minimally invasive\*\* – only a thin needle is used
\*\*Same-day report availability\*\* at Cadabams Diagnostics, saving repeat visits

## Illnesses Diagnosed with MRI Arthrogram Scan

| Condition | What the Scan Shows |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Labral tears (hip/shoulder) | High-signal cleft in labrum |
| Rotator cuff tendon tears | Discontinuity or retraction |
| Articular cartilage defects | Focal thinning or fissures |
| Ligament sprains or ruptures | Ligament fiber disruption |

## Preparing for MRI Arthrogram Scan

### Pre-requisites
• \*\*Fasting\*\*: 4–6 hours for direct arthrograms; none for indirect
• \*\*Medication review\*\*: inform staff of blood thinners or diabetes meds
• \*\*Allergy screening\*\*: disclose any prior contrast reactions
### Best Time to Schedule
Early morning slots reduce waiting time and allow faster post-procedure recovery.
### Eligibility
• Adults and children >14 years
• \*\*Not eligible\*\*: pregnancy, severe kidney disease (eGFR <30), MRI-incompatible implants
### Step-by-Step Procedure
1. Check-in and consent form
2. Vital signs and IV line (if needed)
3. Skin cleaned; local anesthetic applied
4. Contrast injected under fluoroscopic guidance (5–10 min)
5. Transfer to MRI suite (20–30 min scan)
6. Observation for 15 min post-injection
### Cautions Before the Test
• Notify staff of metal implants, pacemakers
• Mild claustrophobia? Ask about open-bore options or light sedation
• Arrange a ride if sedatives are used

faqs.question: Is MRI Peripheral Angio painful?
faqs.answer: No. You may feel a cool sensation during contrast injection—that’s it.

faqs.question: How soon can I resume normal activities?
faqs.answer: Immediately. We only advise 10 minutes of post-scan hydration.

faqs.question: Will I need a driver after the scan?
faqs.answer: Not unless you take sedation. Most patients drive themselves.

faqs.question: What if I have kidney issues?
faqs.answer: Our team reviews your latest eGFR. Non-contrast MRA is available.

faqs.question: Can I eat before the scan?
faqs.answer: For contrast studies, a light 4-hour fast is ideal; plain water is allowed.

faqs.question: What happens after my MRI Peripheral Angio?
faqs.answer: Unless you received sedation, you can resume normal activities and diet immediately after your **MRI Peripheral Angio**. There is no specific recovery time. If contrast is used, it is eliminated naturally through the kidneys. Attend your follow-up appointment with your referring doctor to discuss the scan results and determine next steps in your care plan.

seo.title: MRI Peripheral Angio | Cadabams Diagnostics
seo.description: Learn how MRI Peripheral Angio at Cadabams Diagnostics detects peripheral artery disease without radiation. Fast, painless, same-day slots in Bengaluru.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Peripheral Angio Overview

| Quick Fact | Value |
|------------|-------|
| Scan type | Magnetic Resonance Angiography (MRA) |
| Radiation | Zero |
| Duration | 30–45 minutes |
| Anaesthesia | Not required |
| Same-day report | Available at Cadabams Diagnostics |
### Why choose Cadabams Diagnostics
- \*\*3-Tesla Siemens scanners\*\* give sub-millimetre resolution.
- \*\*ACR-accredited radiologists\*\* report within 60 minutes.
- \*\*Weekend & evening slots\*\* for working professionals.
- \*\*Free pick-up & drop\*\* for seniors in Bengaluru.

## When and Who Needs to Take an MRI Peripheral Angio?

### Common symptoms warranting the test
- Calf pain on walking (claudication).
- Cold, numb or discoloured toes.
- Non-healing ulcers or gangrene.
- Asymmetric leg pulse strength.
### Risk factors for peripheral vascular disease
- Diabetes & hypertension.
- Smoking history.
- High cholesterol or triglycerides.
- Family history of heart attack or stroke before age 55.
### Doctor referral vs direct booking
You can \*\*book directly\*\* if you have a prescription or lab request. If you’re unsure, Cadabams Diagnostics offers a \*\*complimentary vascular risk screening\*\* to help you decide.

## Risks & Limitations of the MRI Peripheral Angio

### Contraindications
- \*\*Permanent pacemaker\*\* or ICD (some newer MRI-conditional devices allowed).
- \*\*Cochlear implants\*\* or older aneurysm clips.
- \*\*Metallic foreign bodies\*\* in eyes or orbital region.
### Contrast agent considerations
- Modern \*\*gadolinium-based agents\*\* are safe for most kidneys.
- Severe \*\*eGFR < 30 ml/min\*\* may need non-contrast protocol.
### Claustrophobia management
- \*\*Open-bore 70 cm wide magnet\*\* reduces anxiety.
- \*\*Light oral sedation\*\* (alprazolam) provided on request.

## List of Parameters Considered During the MRI Peripheral Angio

- \*\*Arterial lumen diameter\*\* – measured in millimetres.
- \*\*Stenosis grading\*\* – percentage reduction in lumen.
- \*\*Collateral vessel visualization\*\* – alternative blood routes.
- \*\*Run-off vessel assessment\*\* – patency of distal foot arteries.

## Reasons for Taking an MRI Peripheral Angio

- \*\*Peripheral artery disease detection\*\* – early before limb loss.
- \*\*Pre-surgical mapping\*\* – bypass or stent planning.
- \*\*Post-intervention follow-up\*\* – check graft or stent patency.
- \*\*Diabetic foot evaluation\*\* – identify micro-vascular disease.

## Advantages of the MRI Peripheral Angiography Scan

- \*\*No ionizing radiation\*\*—safe for repeated follow-ups.
- \*\*High-resolution 3D imaging\*\*—rotation from any angle.
- \*\*Outpatient procedure\*\*—return to work the same day.
- \*\*Short recovery time\*\*—no wound or groin compression.

## Illnesses Diagnosed with MRI Peripheral Angio

| Condition | How MRI Helps |
|-----------|---------------|
| \*\*Peripheral artery disease (PAD)\*\* | Quantifies plaque volume & calcification. |
| \*\*Arteriovenous malformations\*\* | Shows nidus size and feeding vessels. |
| \*\*Thromboangiitis obliterans\*\* | Detects segmental occlusions in young smokers. |
| \*\*Popliteal artery entrapment syndrome\*\* | Dynamic imaging during plantar flexion. |

## Preparing for MRI Peripheral Angio

- \*\*Fasting\*\*: 4 hours for contrast studies; water allowed.
- \*\*Medication\*\*: Take usual blood-pressure drugs; stop metformin if eGFR < 45.
- \*\*Metal screening\*\*: Remove jewellery, hairpins, hearing aids.
---
## Pre-requisites
- \*\*Kidney function tests\*\*: Creatinine and eGFR within 4 weeks.
- \*\*MRI safety questionnaire\*\*: Fill online before arrival.
- \*\*Consent form details\*\*: Overview of risks, contrast usage and data privacy.
---
## Best Time to Take the MRI Peripheral Angio
- \*\*Morning slots\*\* suit fasting patients.
- \*\*Avoid menstruation days\*\* if heavy flow (contrast tolerance lower).
- \*\*Same-day availability\*\* at Cadabams Diagnostics—call 080-1234 5678 before 11 a.m.
---
## Eligibility
- \*\*Age criteria\*\*: 12 years and above.
- \*\*Weight limits\*\*: Up to 180 kg on wide-bore table.
- \*\*Pregnancy considerations\*\*: Avoid contrast in first trimester; non-contrast MRA possible.
---
## Procedure for Taking a MRI Peripheral Angio
1. \*\*Arrival\*\* (15 min early) – check-in and gown change.
2. \*\*IV line\*\* – placed in arm for contrast.
3. \*\*Positioning\*\* – feet first, knees supported with cushions.
4. \*\*Scan\*\* – series of loud thumping sounds; stay still.
5. \*\*Contrast injection\*\* – 10-second warm sensation.
6. \*\*Completion\*\* – IV removed, hydration offered.
7. \*\*Total time\*\* – 30–45 minutes inside scanner.
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- \*\*Inform about allergies\*\* – shellfish or prior contrast reaction.
- \*\*Remove all metallic objects\*\* – rings, belts, phones.
- \*\*Sedation options\*\* – request when booking if claustrophobic.

seo.title: MRI EXTREMITIS WITHOUT CONTRAST at Cadabams Diagnostics | Fast & Safe
seo.description: Learn how MRI EXTREMITIS WITHOUT CONTRAST at Cadabams Diagnostics gives clear limb images without injections. Book online, get same-day digital reports.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Extremities Without Contrast Overview

- \*\*What it is:\*\* A high-field magnetic resonance scan focused on limbs—no contrast injection required.
- \*\*Why preferred:\*\* Superior soft-tissue contrast, zero radiation, and almost no allergy risk.
- \*\*Typical use:\*\* Sports injuries, joint pain, fracture follow-ups, and pre-surgery planning.

## Risks & Limitations of the MRI Extremities Without Contrast

- \*\*No exposure\*\* to iodine or gadolinium—ideal for patients with kidney disease or contrast allergies.
- \*\*Limitation:\*\* May miss very tiny tumors under 3 mm; contrast could be ordered later if needed.
- \*\*Metal concerns:\*\* Pacemakers, cochlear implants, and some aneurysm clips may be contraindicated. Inform Cadabams staff in advance.

## List of Parameters Considered During the MRI Extremities Without Contrast

- \*\*Bone integrity:\*\* Occult or stress fractures
- \*\*Ligament & tendon status:\*\* Sprains, partial or complete tears
- \*\*Cartilage thickness:\*\* Early arthritis or focal defects
- \*\*Muscle edema:\*\* Contusion or strain
- \*\*Joint effusion:\*\* Fluid collections indicating inflammation

## Reasons for Taking an MRI Extremities Without Contrast

- Accurate soft-tissue detail
- Zero contrast allergy risk
- Faster appointment slots—often same-day at Cadabams
- Budget-friendly compared to contrast-enhanced studies

## Undergoing an MRI Extremities Without Contrast at Cadabams Diagnostics offers several significant advantages:

- \*\*No injection discomfort\*\*
- \*\*Same-day digital reports\*\* sent to your phone or email
- \*\*High-resolution imaging\*\* with 3T clarity
- \*\*Safe for kidney-compromised patients\*\*

## Illnesses Diagnosed with MRI Extremities Without Contrast

- Ligament tears (ACL, UCL)
- Meniscal injuries
- Stress fractures of tibia or metatarsals
- Osteomyelitis (bone infection)
- Soft-tissue masses (lipomas, ganglion cysts)

## Preparing for MRI Extremities Without Contrast

### Instructions & Prerequisites
- Wear metal-free clothing or change into Cadabams gowns.
- Remove jewellery, watches, and credit cards.
- Bring previous X-rays or CT scans if available.
### Best Time to Take the Scan
Morning slots reduce waiting time and allow same-day reporting.
### Eligibility
- Adults and children above 5 years (pediatric sedation available on request).
- Pregnancy: Generally safe in 2nd & 3rd trimester; consult your obstetrician.
### Procedure
1. Check-in and safety screening (5 min).
2. Positioning of the limb in a dedicated coil (5 min).
3. Scan duration: 20–40 min depending on area.
4. Immediate review by radiologist; digital report within 2 hours.
### Cautions
- Notify staff of pacemakers, implants, or claustrophobia.
- Kidney function test not required for non-contrast studies.

faqs.answer:
If you suffer from claustrophobia, please inform our staff when scheduling your appointment and when you arrive. We can discuss several options:

    *   Sometimes, mild sedation prescribed by your doctor can help you relax.
    *   In some cases, if available and appropriate for the required image quality, an Open MRI might be an alternative.

seo.title: MRI Bilateral Temporal Bone / Inner Ear (No Contrast)
seo.description: Learn how a painless, no-contrast MRI of both temporal bones and inner ears detects hearing loss, vertigo & tumours. Book today at Cadabams Diagnostics.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Bilateral Temporal Bone / Inner Ear Without Contrast Overview

### Quick Snapshot: What the Scan Does
- \*\*Purpose:\*\* Visualises the cochlea, vestibule, semi-circular canals, auditory nerve and surrounding bone structures.
- \*\*No contrast:\*\* Safer for patients with kidney issues or dye allergies.
- \*\*Duration:\*\* 25–35 minutes total appointment time.
### Why Choose Cadabams Diagnostics
- \*\*3T MRI scanners\*\* for sharper detail than standard 1.5T machines.
- \*\*Same-day digital reports\*\* sent to your phone and referring doctor.
- \*\*Comfort-first approach:\*\* Wide-bore design, music headphones and optional mild sedation.

## Risks & Limitations

- \*\*No radiation risk\*\* – MRI uses magnetic fields, not X-rays.
- \*\*Limitations:\*\* Small inflammatory changes may be less visible than with contrast.
- \*\*Contraindications:\*\*
- Pacemakers or cochlear implants (unless MRI-conditional)
- Severe \*\*claustrophobia\*\* (we offer sedation)
- First-trimester pregnancy (discuss with radiologist)

## List of Parameters Considered During This MRI Scan

| Parameter | What We Check | Why It Matters |
|---|---|---|
| Cochlear nerve calibre | Thickness & signal | Rules out compression or tumour |
| Semi-circular canal integrity | Shape & fluid signal | Diagnoses BPPV or canal dehiscence |
| Middle ear ossicles | Malleus, incus, stapes | Detects erosion or fixation |
| Internal auditory canal (IAC) diameter | Width & symmetry | Early sign of acoustic neuroma |

## Reasons for Taking a Bilateral Temporal Bone / Inner Ear MRI Without Contrast

- \*\*Unexplained hearing loss or tinnitus\*\* – identify tumours or malformations.
- \*\*Vertigo or balance disorders\*\* – visualise inner-ear fluid dynamics.
- \*\*Post-traumatic facial palsy\*\* – check for temporal bone fracture.

## Opting for an MRI Bilateral Temporal Bone / Inner Ear Without Contrast at Cadabams Diagnostics offers several significant benefits in your diagnostic journey:

- \*\*Early detection of acoustic neuroma\*\* – improves treatment outcomes.
- \*\*Non-invasive and painless\*\* – no needles or radiation.
- \*\*Same-day digital reports\*\* – faster specialist consultation.

## Illnesses Diagnosed with a Temporal Bone & Inner Ear MRI (Without Contrast)

- \*\*Acoustic neuroma / vestibular schwannoma\*\* – benign nerve-sheath tumour.
- \*\*Meniere’s disease\*\* – endolymphatic hydrops.
- \*\*Labyrinthitis\*\* – viral or bacterial inflammation.
- \*\*Cholesteatoma\*\* – cystic mass eroding bone.

## Preparing for Your MRI Bilateral Temporal Bone / Inner Ear Without Contrast

- \*\*Fasting not required\*\* – eat and drink normally.
- \*\*Remove all metallic objects\*\* – jewellery, hairpins, hearing aids.
- \*\*Inform about implants\*\* – pacemaker, aneurysm clips, or tattoos with metallic ink.
---
## Pre-requisites
- \*\*Signed MRI safety checklist\*\* – completed on arrival.
- \*\*Recent audiometry report\*\* – bring if available; helps correlate findings.
---
## Best Time to Take the MRI Bilateral Temporal Bone / Inner Ear Without Contrast
- \*\*Morning slots\*\* – shorter wait times and fresher staff.
- \*\*Book 24–48 h in advance\*\* – secure your preferred slot via our app or website.
---
## Eligibility
- \*\*Adults and children over 5 years\*\* – paediatric sedation available.
- \*\*No renal function restrictions\*\* – no contrast means no GFR blood test needed.
---
## Procedure for Taking a MRI Bilateral Temporal Bone / Inner Ear Without Contrast
1. \*\*Check-in & safety screening\*\* (~10 min)
2. \*\*Positioning in 3T scanner\*\* – lie on your back, head in a cushioned cradle (~30 min scan)
3. \*\*Post-scan image QC & digital upload\*\* – radiologist reviews images immediately.
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- \*\*Notify staff of pregnancy\*\* – especially first trimester.
- \*\*Claustrophobic patients\*\* – request mild oral sedation (requires accompanying adult).

faqs.question: Is an MRI Leg painful?
faqs.answer: No. You only need to lie still; there is no pain or injection unless contrast is used.

faqs.question: Can I eat before the scan?
faqs.answer: Yes, for a non-contrast MRI Leg. Fast 4 hours if contrast is planned.

faqs.question: How soon can I get results?
faqs.answer: Reports are available within 24 hours at Cadabams Diagnostics.

faqs.question: Is the scan safe for kids?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. MRI uses no radiation, making it safe for children.

faqs.question: What if I’m claustrophobic?
faqs.answer: We offer open-bore MRI machines and mild sedation options.

faqs.question: Are there any side effects or after-effects from a Leg MRI?
faqs.answer: The **Leg MRI** scan itself, using magnetic fields and radio waves, has no known persistent side effects. Most people can resume their normal activities immediately after the scan. If a contrast dye was used for your **Leg MRI**, there is a small risk of an allergic reaction (usually mild, like a rash or nausea, but rarely more serious). If you received sedation for claustrophobia or inability to lie still, you might feel groggy for a few hours and will need someone to drive you home. Otherwise, there are generally no after-effects from the **leg MRI scan procedure**.

seo.title: MRI Leg at Cadabams Diagnostics: Quick & Accurate
seo.description: Discover how an MRI Leg scan at Cadabams Diagnostics delivers clear, radiation-free images to diagnose injuries, pain, and hidden conditions in minutes.

relatedTests.markdown:
## MRI Leg Overview

### What is an MRI Leg?
An \*\*MRI Leg\*\* is a painless scan that uses powerful magnets and radio waves to create detailed pictures of bones, muscles, tendons, ligaments, and blood vessels in your leg.
### How it differs from X-ray or CT
- \*\*X-ray\*\* shows bones only and uses radiation.
- \*\*CT scan\*\* uses radiation and is better for bone detail.
- \*\*MRI Leg\*\* shows \*\*soft tissues\*\* clearly and has \*\*no radiation\*\*.

## Risks & Limitations of the Leg MRI

### Contrast allergy risks
- Rare reaction to gadolinium contrast dye
- Inform staff of any previous allergies
### Claustrophobia considerations
- Open-bore MRI machines available
- Mild sedation offered if needed
### Metal implant restrictions
- Pacemakers, cochlear implants, or metal fragments may limit scan
- Full screening done before appointment

## List of Parameters Considered During the Leg MRI

- \*\*Bone integrity\*\* – detects fractures, bone bruises
- \*\*Ligament & tendon status\*\* – checks for tears or inflammation
- \*\*Cartilage thickness\*\* – evaluates joint wear (osteoarthritis)
- \*\*Soft-tissue masses\*\* – identifies cysts, tumors, or abscesses

## Reasons for Taking a Leg MRI

- \*\*Unexplained pain\*\* – when X-rays appear normal
- \*\*Swelling evaluation\*\* – to rule out infection or clot
- \*\*Post-injury assessment\*\* – to plan surgery or rehab

## Benefits of Taking the Test

- \*\*No radiation\*\* – safe for children and pregnant women (after first trimester)
- \*\*High-resolution images\*\* – shows millimeter-level detail
- \*\*Early detection of pathologies\*\* – catches problems before they worsen

## Illnesses Diagnosed with Leg MRI

- \*\*Stress fractures\*\* – tiny cracks from overuse
- \*\*ACL/PCL tears\*\* – knee ligament injuries
- \*\*Osteoarthritis\*\* – cartilage loss and bone spurs
- \*\*Tumors or cysts\*\* – benign or malignant growths

## Preparing for Leg MRI

### Instructions
- Wear loose, metal-free clothing
- Remove jewelry, watches, and hairpins
- Fast 4 hours only if contrast is planned
### Prerequisites
- Bring previous reports and doctor’s prescription
- Fill out safety screening form
### Best time to take the scan
- Morning slots reduce waiting time
- Avoid scheduling during acute pain flare-ups
### Eligibility
- Adults and children above 5 years
- Pregnant women after first trimester (non-contrast)
---
## Procedure for Taking an MRI Leg
### Step-by-step guide
1. Change into hospital gown if needed.
2. Lie on the scanner table; leg is positioned in a coil.
3. Earplugs or headphones reduce machine noise.
4. Stay still for 20–40 minutes.
5. Contrast dye is injected halfway if required.
### Duration and what to expect
- \*\*20–40 minutes\*\* total
- Loud knocking sounds are normal
- No pain; slight warmth if contrast is used
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- \*\*Remove metal objects\*\* – keys, coins, belts
- \*\*Inform about implants\*\* – pacemaker, screws, or plates
- \*\*Pregnancy considerations\*\* – notify staff if pregnant or breastfeeding
